response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a seasoned generalist software developer with strong web and backend credentials, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role on every critical dimension: there is no Japanese language proficiency (a hard, non-negotiable requirement), no embedded networking or security domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, NETCONF, SNMP are entirely absent), and no Linux kernel development experience. Their C exposure is minimal and incidental — a brief stint at NI and a thesis project — not the deep, UNIX-systems-level C proficiency the role demands. The profile is that of a TypeScript/Node.js web developer, not an embedded firmware security engineer.
strengths: 15+ years of continuous software development experience across diverse domains and tech stacks
Demonstrated C and C++ exposure through NI LabWindows work and a wave-field synthesis thesis project
Consistent Linux usage across multiple roles, indicating familiarity with the OS environment
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for this position
Zero experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, MIB/SNMP, NETCONF, switch chip SDKs)
No Linux kernel investigation or source-level modification experience; career is overwhelmingly web and application-layer focused

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
